Find  $m+n.$', initial_info={'experiment_name': 'orz57k-notool', 'task_model_id': 'bedrock/us.meta.llama3-1-8b-instruct-v1:0', 'episode_index': 0}, steps=[TrajectoryStep(step_index=1, action='We have that $x+\frac{1}{z}=5$ and $y+\frac{1}{x}=29$. Multiplying the first equation by $z$ and the second equation by $x$ gives $xz+1=5z$ and $xy+1=29x$. Subtracting the first equation from the second equation gives $xy-xz=28x-5z$. Factoring out $x$ and $z$ gives $x(y-z)=28x-5z$. Factoring out $x$ and $z$ again gives $x(z-28)=5z$. Dividing both sides by $z-28$ gives $x=\frac{5z}{z-28}$. We can rewrite this as $x=\frac{5z}{z-28}=\frac{5z(z-28+28)}{z(z-28)}=\frac{5z^2-140z}{z^2-28z}$. We can rewrite this as $x=\frac{5z^2-140z}{z(z-28)}=\frac{5z(z-28)-140z}{z(z-28)}=\frac{5z^2-140z-140z}{z(z-28)}=\frac{5z^2-280z}{z(z-28)}$. We can rewrite this as $x=\frac{5z^2-280z}{z(z-28)}=\frac{5z(z-56)}{z(z-28)}$. Cancelling out the common factor of $z$ gives $x=\frac{5(z-56)}{(z-28)}$. We can rewrite this as $x=\frac{5(z-56)}{(z-28)}=\frac{5(z-28-28)}{(z-28)}=\frac{5(z-28)(1-28/5)}{(z-28)}=\frac{5(z-28)(5/5-28/5)}{(z-28)}=\frac{5(z-28)(-23/5)}{(z-28)}$. Cancelling out the common factor of $(z-28)$ gives $x=\frac{5(z-28)(-23/5)}{(z-28)}=-23$.

We have that $x=-23$ and $y+\frac{1}{x}=29$. Substituting $x=-23$ into the second equation gives $y+\frac{1}{-23}=29$. Multiplying both sides by $-23$ gives $-23y-1=667$. Adding $1$ to both sides gives $-23y=668$. Dividing both sides by $-23$ gives $y=-\frac{668}{23}=-\frac{668}{23}=-\frac{4\cdot 167}{23}=-\frac{4\cdot 167}{23}=-4\cdot \frac{167}{23}=-4\cdot \frac{167}{23}=-4\cdot \frac{167}{23}=-4\cdot
